scenario_name: Scenario 16 — Distributed System Failure in Code
justification: Kobe correctly identifies all 3 failure modes with detailed analysis, identifies the saga-without-compensation pattern, proposes specific compensating transactions including courierService.release(courierId), and sets safe_to_deploy false. However, the escalations array is empty — there is no escalation to MJ for saga design review, which is a required rubric criterion for pass.

observations.text: Courier stuck in reserved state (step 1 success, step 2 failure) explicitly identified as Critical with clear reproduction steps
All 3 failure modes analyzed in detail across the critical findings
Saga pattern clearly identified with specific compensating transaction recommendations including courierService.release(courierId)
safe_to_deploy is false with appropriate rollback plan
Goes beyond rubric requirements by identifying idempotency issues and cascading latency concerns
escalations array is empty — no MJ escalation for saga design review, which is an explicit pass criterion
The saga orchestrator recommendation appears only in suggestions, not as a formal escalation to MJ as required

agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Multi-step distributed operation with zero error handling guarantees orphaned state on any partial failure"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Partial failure leaves irrecoverable orphaned state","risk":"If any step after `reserve()` fails, the system enters an inconsistent state with no rollback. A reserved courier is never released, an order may show ASSIGNED with no dispatch sent, or a courier dispatches with no customer notification. Each failure point

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
field confidence.level value 95 is above max 75
